title: pittosporum xenicum
intro: Pittosporum xenicum, also known as New Zealand tobacco tree or Kohuhu, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Pittosporum xenicum is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
